description: Local Android self-surveillance app: collects the categories trackers harvest, stores only in SQLCipher, zero network egress of collected data. Build on top of existing scaffold (Collector interface, Ingestor, MirrorDatabase, CryptoManager, 2 reference collectors already wired).

context.scaffold: Assume the scaffold at mirrortrack-scaffold.tar.gz is already extracted and committed. Read CLAUDE.md before starting any story.
context.stack: Kotlin 2.1, AGP 8.7, Compose BOM 2024.11, Room 2.6.1, SQLCipher 4.6.1, Hilt 2.52, Argon2kt 1.6
rules: INTERNET permission consumed only by US-033 (IpCollector). Any other network use is a rejected PR.
New collectors ship with defaultEnabled = false.
No android.util.Log in non-DEBUG code paths.
Passphrases are CharArray, not String; zeroed after use.
Every collector registered in CollectorRegistryModule.provideCollectors explicitly (no @IntoSet).
globalAcceptanceCriteria: No new INTERNET traffic unless story is US-033

userStories.title: Unlock flow: passphrase → Argon2id → DB open
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want to unlock the app with my passphrase so that the encrypted DB is opened and collectors can write to it.
acceptanceCriteria: UnlockScreen (Compose) with masked passphrase field + Unlock button + first-run 'create passphrase' confirm field.
UnlockViewModel (Hilt) calls CryptoManager.deriveKey(CharArray), passes rawKey to DatabaseHolder.open(), catches SQLiteException on wrong key and shows 'incorrect passphrase'.
Passphrase CharArray is zeroed in the ViewModel within 100ms of deriveKey return.
On success, NavHost routes to the LiveFeed destination; on failure, field is cleared and focus returns.
Typecheck passes; ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug succeeds.

userStories.title: App shell: NavHost with unlock-gated routes
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want a single-activity navigation host so that I can move between Unlock, LiveFeed, CategoryBrowser, and Settings.
acceptanceCriteria: NavHost with routes: unlock, feed, categories, category/{id}, datapoint/{rowId}, settings, permissions.
Start destination = unlock if DatabaseHolder.isOpen() is false, else feed.
BackHandler on feed prompts before exiting to unlock (which closes DB).
Material 3 scaffold with bottom nav (Feed, Categories, Settings) visible only post-unlock.
Typecheck passes; app launches, unlocks, shows feed placeholder.

userStories.title: LiveFeedScreen: observe recent DataPoints
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want a reverse-chronological live feed of collected DataPoints so that I can see what is being recorded in real time.
acceptanceCriteria: LiveFeedScreen observes dao.observeRecent(500) via collectAsStateWithLifecycle.
Each row shows: relative timestamp, category icon, collectorId, key, truncated value (40 chars).
Tapping a row navigates to datapoint/{rowId} detail route.
Empty state: 'No data yet. Enable collectors in Settings.'
LazyColumn with keys = rowId; no visible jank at 1000+ rows.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: PermissionsScreen: runtime permission request helper
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want a dedicated screen that explains and requests Android runtime permissions so that I can grant only what I want.
acceptanceCriteria: Screen lists every RUNTIME-tier permission referenced by any registered Collector with its rationale string.
Per-permission toggle uses rememberLauncherForActivityResult(ActivityResultContracts.RequestPermission).
Denied-twice state shows a 'Open app settings' link via Intent.ACTION_APPLICATION_DETAILS_SETTINGS.
Shows current grant state live (recomposes on resume).
Does NOT request permissions the user hasn't enabled a collector for.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: CollectionForegroundService with dignified notification
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want a foreground service so that collection continues in the background without being killed by Android's Doze.
acceptanceCriteria: Service class declared in manifest with foregroundServiceType=specialUse (already in manifest).
Builds NotificationChannel IMPORTANCE_LOW with description from strings.xml.
Notification text uses format '{N} streams · {M} points today' updated every 60s.
Service.startIfEnabled(context) static method checks DataStore flag before starting.
onStartCommand returns START_STICKY.
Tapping notification opens MainActivity.
Service respects DatabaseHolder lock state — does not invoke collectors if DB closed.
Typecheck passes; service visible in adb shell dumpsys activity services.

userStories.title: CollectionScheduler: WorkManager driver for polled collectors
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want polled collectors to run at their configured cadence so that device state is sampled over time.
acceptanceCriteria: HiltWorker class PolledCollectionWorker reads CollectorRegistry.all(), filters isEnabled && defaultPollInterval != null && isAvailable(context), calls collect(), submits to Ingestor.

One PeriodicWorkRequest scheduled per collector, named by collectorId, with ExistingPeriodicWorkPolicy.UPDATE, interval = collector's configured pollInterval (default or user override from DataStore).
Scheduler.refreshAll() rebuilds the queue when user toggles collectors.
Worker catches and logs per-collector exceptions without killing the batch.
Minimum interval clamped to 15 min per WorkManager contract; collectors needing faster cadence must be streamed.
Typecheck passes; BuildInfoCollector writes to DB on first scheduled run.

userStories.title: Streamed collector runtime in ForegroundService
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want streamed collectors (screen state, sensors) to emit continuously so that event-driven data is captured.
acceptanceCriteria: CollectionForegroundService holds a SupervisorJob scope; launches one coroutine per enabled streamed collector.
Each coroutine collects collector.stream(context) and forwards to Ingestor.submit.
Collector enable/disable toggles (re)launch or cancel the matching coroutine within 2 seconds.
On service destroy, scope.cancel() runs awaitClose in every callbackFlow and unregisters receivers.
ScreenStateCollector produces DataPoints visible in LiveFeed on screen on/off.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: CategoryBrowser + DataPointDetail
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want to drill into a category and then a specific DataPoint so that I can inspect what was collected.
acceptanceCriteria: CategoryBrowser lists Category.values() with row count per category (dao.countByCollector aggregated).
Tapping a category shows all DataPoints for that category, grouped by collector, newest first, paginated at 200.
DataPointDetail shows full row: absolute timestamp, collectorId, category, key, full value (scrollable), valueType.
JSON valueType values are pretty-printed with kotlinx.serialization.json.Json { prettyPrint = true }.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Per-collector settings screen (enable, pollInterval override)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want to toggle each collector on/off and adjust its poll interval so that I control what is collected and how often.
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsScreen lists every collector grouped by Category with: toggle switch, rationale, current state (available/needs-permission/running).
Long-press on polled collector reveals pollInterval slider (15m, 1h, 6h, 24h presets).
State stored in DataStore under keys collector.<id>.enabled and collector.<id>.pollIntervalMinutes.
Toggling enabled=true for a RUNTIME-tier collector that lacks permission triggers PermissionsScreen.
Toggling enabled=false cancels any active stream job within 2 seconds.
Scheduler.refreshAll() called on every toggle change.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: RetentionPolicy + periodic purge worker
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want per-collector TTL so that high-frequency sensor data doesn't fill the DB indefinitely.
acceptanceCriteria: Collector interface gains `val defaultRetention: Duration? get() = null` (null = keep forever).
BuildInfoCollector defaultRetention = 365.days; ScreenStateCollector defaultRetention = 90.days; sensor collectors (future) default 7.days.
RetentionWorker (PeriodicWorkRequest, 24h) iterates CollectorRegistry.all(), calls dao.purgeOlderThan(id, now - ttl).
Per-collector TTL overridable in Settings via DataStore.
Worker logs total rows purged to logcat in DEBUG; silent in release.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: BatteryCollector (hybrid: sticky broadcast + interval)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want battery level/state/temp collected continuously so that I have the same signal trackers use for device health fingerprinting.
acceptanceCriteria: Hybrid: stream() subscribes to ACTION_BATTERY_CHANGED sticky; also polls every 5 min as a fallback.
Emits DataPoints for: level (0–100), scale, is_charging, charge_plug, status, health, temperature_tenths_c, voltage_mv, technology.
Registered in CollectorRegistryModule.
defaultEnabled = true (zero-risk, no permission).
DataPoints visible in LiveFeed when user plugs/unplugs charger.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: IntegrityCollector (root, emulator, debugger heuristics)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want the same device-integrity signals trackers compute so that I can see my own device's profile.
acceptanceCriteria: Polled, 24h cadence.

Emits: rooted_heuristic (bool), su_binary_present (bool), test_keys (bool from Build.TAGS), emulator_heuristic (bool from Build.FINGERPRINT/HARDWARE/PRODUCT), debugger_attached (bool from Debug.isDebuggerConnected), adb_enabled (bool from Settings.Global.ADB_ENABLED), developer_options (bool), play_integrity_attestation (string or 'unavailable').
Play Integrity call is best-effort and does NOT add play-services-* to deps (use raw IntegrityManager if available via compileOnly, else emit 'unavailable').
No play-services-* artifact added.
Typecheck passes; new entries appear in LiveFeed after first poll.

userStories.title: IdentifiersCollector (ANDROID_ID, install UUID, GAID if any)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want the persistent identifiers trackers key off of so that I can see my fingerprint.
acceptanceCriteria: Polled, 24h cadence.

Emits: android_id (Settings.Secure.ANDROID_ID, scoped to our signing key — note this in rationale), install_uuid (generated on first run, stored in DataStore, stable for life of install), first_install_time (from PackageManager), last_update_time, package_signing_sha256 (hex of first signer cert).
GAID intentionally NOT collected unless user explicitly enables a sub-toggle (it requires Play Services and Google is deprecating it).
Rationale string explicitly calls out that ANDROID_ID is per-signing-key scoped.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: HardwareCollector (RAM, storage, CPU, display)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want hardware characteristics recorded so that I see the same device-fingerprint trackers compile.
acceptanceCriteria: Polled, 24h cadence.

Emits: total_ram_bytes, available_ram_bytes (ActivityManager.MemoryInfo), total_storage_bytes, available_storage_bytes (StatFs on internal), cpu_abi_list (json), cpu_core_count, screen_width_px, screen_height_px, density_dpi, refresh_rate_hz, font_scale, hdr_capabilities (json), dark_mode (bool from Configuration.uiMode).
No permission required.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: MotionSensorCollector (accel, gyro, mag)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want periodic motion-sensor snapshots so that I capture what movement-based fingerprinting sees.
acceptanceCriteria: Streamed. Subscribes at SENSOR_DELAY_NORMAL (~5Hz) but internally downsamples to 1Hz before emitting (batching timer).
Emits per-sample DataPoints for: accel_x/y/z, gyro_x/y/z, mag_x/y/z.
Only subscribes if the sensor is present (SensorManager.getDefaultSensor != null).
Unregisters on Flow cancellation.
No permission required for these sensors.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: EnvironmentSensorCollector (light, proximity, pressure, temp)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want environment sensor snapshots so that ambient-context signals are recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: Streamed, downsampled to 0.2Hz (one sample per 5s).
Emits: ambient_light_lux, proximity_cm, barometric_pressure_hpa, ambient_temperature_c when those sensors exist.
Gracefully degrades: emits nothing for absent sensors rather than throwing.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: StepCollector (Sensor.TYPE_STEP_COUNTER + STEP_DETECTOR)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want step counts logged so that I see activity-level signals.
acceptanceCriteria: Streamed. STEP_COUNTER is cumulative since boot — emit delta since last sample, not raw counter.
STEP_DETECTOR emits one DataPoint per detected step event.
Requires ACTIVITY_RECOGNITION on A10+. Collector's accessTier = RUNTIME.
Handles reboot (counter resets) by detecting decrease and emitting reset marker.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: ConnectivityCollector
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want connection type and transport changes recorded so that network fingerprint shifts are tracked.
acceptanceCriteria: Hybrid: polled 5m cadence + ConnectivityManager.NetworkCallback for change events.
Emits: active_transport (wifi/cellular/ethernet/vpn/none), is_metered, is_validated, link_downstream_kbps, link_upstream_kbps, vpn_active (bool).
Uses ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE (already in manifest, no runtime prompt).
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: CarrierCollector
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want carrier/SIM fingerprint data captured so that regional signals trackers read are mirrored.
acceptanceCriteria: Polled 24h.
Emits: carrier_name, mcc, mnc, sim_country_iso, sim_operator, network_country_iso, network_operator, phone_type, is_roaming.
Gracefully handles devices without telephony hardware (emits 'unavailable').
No READ_PHONE_STATE needed for these specific fields on modern APIs.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: AppLifecycleCollector (our own app sessions)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want our app's own lifecycle recorded so that session-tracking-SDK behavior is mirrored for our app.
acceptanceCriteria: Streamed. Uses ProcessLifecycleOwner to detect app_foreground / app_background.
Emits session_start (foreground) with session_uuid, session_end (background) with duration_ms.
Session UUID persists across brief backgrounds (<5s) to match typical SDK behavior.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: WifiCollector (SSID, BSSID, RSSI, scan results)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want wifi environment captured so that location-adjacent fingerprinting is mirrored.
acceptanceCriteria: accessTier = RUNTIME. Requires ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION on A9+ for SSID/BSSID; NEARBY_WIFI_DEVICES on A13+ for scan without location.
Polled 15m cadence.
Emits current_ssid, current_bssid, current_rssi_dbm, current_link_speed_mbps, current_frequency_mhz.
Scan result list emitted as JSON array per row: [{ssid, bssid, rssi, frequency, capabilities}, ...].
isAvailable returns false when permission missing; collector appears disabled in UI.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: LocationCollector (raw LocationManager, no FusedLocationProvider)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want GPS/network-fix locations so that geolocation signal is mirrored — without Google Play Services.
acceptanceCriteria: Uses android.location.LocationManager directly. No play-services-location dependency.
accessTier = RUNTIME. Requires ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION.
Streamed. Subscribes to GPS_PROVIDER + NETWORK_PROVIDER with 5-minute min-time / 50m min-distance.
Emits: lat, lon, altitude_m, accuracy_m, bearing_deg, speed_mps, provider, is_mock.
Handles LocationManager.PASSIVE_PROVIDER for opportunistic sampling.
Background location (ACCESS_BACKGROUND_LOCATION) requested only if user explicitly enables background collection in Settings.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: ActivityRecognitionCollector (manual HAR from accel)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want activity classification (walking/still/driving) without Play Services.
acceptanceCriteria: Since ActivityRecognitionClient lives in play-services, implement a lightweight rule-based classifier over the last 30s of MotionSensorCollector data.
Emits: activity (still/walking/running/vehicle/unknown), confidence_estimate (0–1).
Classifier is a simple heuristic: RMS of accel magnitude → state; documented in class header with explicit 'this is a toy classifier, not ML'.
accessTier = RUNTIME (ACTIVITY_RECOGNITION permission).
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: BodySensorsCollector (heart rate if hardware present)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want heart rate samples when the device has the sensor.
acceptanceCriteria: Streamed at 0.1Hz (every 10s).
Subscribes to Sensor.TYPE_HEART_RATE if present.
accessTier = RUNTIME (BODY_SENSORS permission).
isAvailable returns false on phones without heart rate hardware.
Emits: heart_rate_bpm, accuracy_level.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: BluetoothCollector (paired + scan)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want nearby and paired Bluetooth device fingerprints recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: accessTier = RUNTIME. BLUETOOTH_SCAN + BLUETOOTH_CONNECT on A12+.
Polled 30m cadence.
Emits paired_devices JSON list (name, address_per_app_random, type, bond_state) + scan_results JSON list (address, rssi, name_if_any) from a 10s LE scan.
Scan uses neverForLocation flag; no location permission triggered.
Stops scan on collector disable.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: ContactsCollector (hashed + full toggle)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want contact-list fingerprint captured in either hashed-count or full mode.
acceptanceCriteria: accessTier = RUNTIME. READ_CONTACTS.
Two sub-modes in settings: 'count + hashed set' (default) and 'full dump'.
Hashed mode: emits contact_count, unique_hashed_phones_count, unique_hashed_emails_count — hash = SHA256 of normalized value.
Full mode: emits one DataPoint per contact with {name, phones[], emails[], starred, times_contacted, last_contacted}.
Polled 24h.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: CalendarCollector
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want calendar events recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: accessTier = RUNTIME. READ_CALENDAR.
Polled 6h.
Emits, per event in the next ±30 days: calendar_account, title, location, start_ms, end_ms, all_day, attendee_count, organizer_email.
One DataPoint per event, keyed by event_id.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: MediaExifCollector
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want EXIF from photos captured so that I see what media-library trackers read.
acceptanceCriteria: accessTier = RUNTIME. READ_MEDIA_IMAGES (A13+) / READ_EXTERNAL_STORAGE (older).
Polled 24h. Processes only photos created since last run (watermark in DataStore).
Emits per image: media_id, date_taken, mime_type, width, height, size_bytes, exif_gps_lat, exif_gps_lon, exif_camera_make, exif_camera_model, exif_software, exif_iso, exif_exposure_time.
Uses ExifInterface via content resolver InputStream; does NOT copy full image.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: UsageStatsCollector with onboarding deep-link
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want per-app foreground time captured so that I mirror the behavioral signal session SDKs compute.
acceptanceCriteria: accessTier = SPECIAL_ACCESS. PACKAGE_USAGE_STATS.
Onboarding screen explains the permission and deep-links to Settings.ACTION_USAGE_ACCESS_SETTINGS.
Polled 1h cadence. Emits per-package DataPoints: total_foreground_ms, last_used_ms, launch_count, first_timestamp, app_standby_bucket.
isAvailable returns false when AppOpsManager.checkOpNoThrow(OPSTR_GET_USAGE_STATS) != MODE_ALLOWED.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: NotificationListenerCollector
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want posted-notification metadata captured so that I see what NLS-based trackers see.
acceptanceCriteria: accessTier = SPECIAL_ACCESS. BIND_NOTIFICATION_LISTENER_SERVICE.
Onboarding screen deep-links to Settings.ACTION_NOTIFICATION_LISTENER_SETTINGS.
MirrorNotificationListenerService emits on onNotificationPosted: package, post_time, category, priority, title_present (bool), text_present (bool), action_count.
Default mode records metadata only, NOT title/text body. A settings toggle 'record notification contents' enables full body capture with a scary red warning.
isAvailable checks NotificationManagerCompat.getEnabledListenerPackages().
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: PrivacyDashboardCollector (A12+ PermissionManager.getPermissionUsages)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want to see which other apps recently accessed sensors/camera/location so that I audit their behavior from within this app.
acceptanceCriteria: accessTier = NONE on A12+; unavailable on A11 and below.
Polled 6h cadence.
Calls PermissionManager.getPermissionUsages via reflection (API is @SystemApi on some paths — handle missing gracefully).
Emits per (package, permission_group) pair: last_access_ms, access_count_7d.
Typecheck passes; falls back gracefully on A11.

userStories.title: InstalledAppsCollector
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want the full installed-app list so that I mirror what QUERY_ALL_PACKAGES trackers enumerate.
acceptanceCriteria: accessTier = RESTRICTED (Play Store policy flag). QUERY_ALL_PACKAGES.
Polled 24h.
Emits per package: package_name, version_name, version_code, install_time, update_time, install_source, signing_sha256, permissions_requested (json), permissions_granted (json), is_system, enabled.
Warning shown in Settings on first enable: 'This permission is Play Store restricted. If you plan to publish to Play Store, this permission requires justification.'
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: IpCollector (the one audited network-egress collector)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want my public IP recorded so that my external-facing network fingerprint is mirrored.
acceptanceCriteria: Polled 1h cadence. defaultEnabled = false.
HTTP GET to a configurable endpoint (default https://api.ipify.org?format=text) using HttpURLConnection; NO OkHttp / Retrofit / Ktor.
Timeout 5s connect / 5s read.
Emits: public_ip_v4 (or v6), lookup_endpoint, lookup_status_code.
Rationale string explicitly calls out: 'This is the only collector that makes any outbound network request. Disable if you want true zero egress.'
Network call routed through the app's own VPN-less HTTP — logged to logcat in DEBUG so the user can see it happen.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: ExportManager (encrypted tarball with KDF params manifest)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want to export the DB for desktop analysis without losing the ability to decrypt it later.
acceptanceCriteria: Export writes a .tar.gz to a user-chosen Storage Access Framework URI containing: mirrortrack.db (SQLCipher bytes), salt.bin (16 bytes), manifest.json ({kdf:'argon2id', t:int, m:int, p:int, app_version_code:int, export_ts_ms:long}).
Ingestor.flush() called before export so no pending rows are missed.
manifest.json's kdf params MUST match CryptoManager current constants; if they ever diverge, CryptoManager reads the manifest to re-derive on the desktop side.
Desktop decrypt.py is updated to read manifest.json instead of hardcoding params (update desktop-analysis/decrypt.py).
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Panic wipe (panic PIN silently purges DB)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want a panic PIN that silently wipes the DB rather than unlocking it.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings has an optional 'Set panic PIN' field (separate from the main passphrase).

On UnlockScreen, if entered value matches the panic PIN hash, the app: deletes the DB file, deletes salt, clears DataStore, then shows the normal 'first run — create passphrase' UI as if fresh install.
Panic PIN is stored hashed (Argon2id over the PIN with a separate salt) in DataStore — never plaintext.
No logcat entry, no toast, no visual artifact on panic path in release builds.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Android Keystore wrapping of DB key (biometric required)
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want the DB key additionally protected by Android Keystore so that a rooted attacker with the encrypted DB cannot brute-force it without the device biometric.
acceptanceCriteria: CryptoManager gains wrapKey(rawKey) and unwrapKey(wrappedBlob) methods using KeyGenParameterSpec with setUserAuthenticationRequired(true) + setUserAuthenticationParameters(0, AUTHENTICATION_TYPE_BIOMETRIC_STRONG).
After first-run passphrase setup, the Argon2-derived key is wrapped by a Keystore AES/GCM/NoPadding key and stored in DataStore.
On subsequent unlocks: BiometricPrompt → unlock Keystore key → unwrap → open DB. Fallback to passphrase entry if biometric fails.
Passphrase change flow re-derives and re-wraps.
Feature gated behind Settings toggle 'Enable biometric unlock'. Off by default (opt-in).
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: Self-audit surfacing
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want the app to flag its own unexpected behavior so that I can trust it.
acceptanceCriteria: A SelfAuditView on the Settings screen shows: declared permissions (from PackageManager), permissions actually used by at least one enabled collector, per-collector write counts in last 24h.
Any declared permission with no enabled consumer is highlighted red with 'Unused — consider removing from manifest'.
Shows build flavor, version, commit hash (from BuildConfig), and installer source.
Typecheck passes.

userStories.title: 'What a tracker would see' simulator export
userStories.description: As the device owner, I want a one-click JSON export mimicking what a typical ads SDK would POST so that I can inspect my fingerprint concretely.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings > 'Simulate tracker payload' generates a JSON file at the SAF URI with fields grouped like a plausible ads-SDK payload: {device: {...}, network: {...}, app: {...}, user: {...}}.
Values come from the latest DataPoint per relevant (collectorId, key) in the DB.
Output includes a 'disclaimer' field: 'This file was generated locally from your own DB. It was never transmitted.'
No network egress during generation.
Typecheck passes.

v1_ready_when: US-001 through US-010 all passes = true (foundation complete)
US-011 through US-020 all passes = true (Tier NONE collectors complete)
US-021 through US-028 passes = true (Tier RUNTIME complete)
US-029 through US-032 pass if targeting F-Droid; optional for Play Store release
US-034 and US-037 pass (export + self-audit — required for real use)
US-035, US-036, US-038 are polish and can ship in v1.1
